skill.name: to-prd
skill.description: Turns the current conversation and codebase context into a product requirements document, then publishes it to the project issue tracker.
skill.summary: Create a PRD from current project context and prepare it for issue triage.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Static command execution and weak cryptography findings appear to be false positives caused by markdown code spans and the word description. The skill has a real medium-risk semantic issue because it instructs the agent to publish synthesized conversation and codebase context to an issue tracker.

medium_findings.title: Issue Tracker Publication Without Final Review
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to synthesize current conversation context and codebase understanding into a PRD, then publish it to the project issue tracker. This can expose sensitive discussion or repository details if the PRD is posted before user review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The publication instruction is explicit, and the source data includes conversation and codebase context. The risk depends on tracker permissions and whether the user reviews the PRD before posting.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Syntax False Positive
low_findings.description: The static command execution hits are markdown inline code and workflow instructions, not Ruby or shell backtick execution. No executable script or shell interpolation is present in the scanned file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is limited to markdown code spans around a slash command and label name. I found no shell command, Ruby code, or executable file in the provided context.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography False Positive
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography hits correspond to ordinary documentation text, including the word description. No cryptographic function, algorithm use, hashing operation, or encryption implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scanner appears to match a weak algorithm substring inside normal prose. I found no evidence of cryptographic behavior in the skill file.

content.value_statement: Teams often lose product decisions in long technical discussions. This skill turns known context into a structured PRD and sends it to triage.
actual_capabilities: Synthesizes the current conversation and codebase understanding into a PRD.
Uses sections for problem, solution, user stories, implementation decisions, testing decisions, out of scope items, and notes.
Prompts the agent to explore the repository and use project glossary terms.
Guides the agent to identify major modules that may need implementation or changes.
Asks for confirmation on module choices and test coverage decisions.
Publishes the PRD to the project issue tracker with the needs-triage label.
limitations: Requires issue tracker details and label vocabulary from setup or prior context.
Does not provide authentication steps or issue tracker API commands.
May publish sensitive context if the user does not review the PRD first.
Avoids specific file paths and code snippets in the PRD by design.

use_cases.title: Convert a feature discussion into a PRD
use_cases.description: Create a structured requirements document from an existing planning conversation.

use_cases.title: Prepare engineering implementation scope
use_cases.description: Capture modules, interfaces, testing choices, and out of scope items before building.

use_cases.title: Send feature work into triage
use_cases.description: Publish a PRD to the project issue tracker with the triage label already applied.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic PRD
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request after discussing a feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the to-prd skill to turn our current discussion into a PRD. Keep the document focused on the feature we discussed.

prompt_templates.title: Create a PRD after repo review
prompt_templates.scenario: Planning work that needs codebase context
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the to-prd skill. First review the relevant project structure, then write a PRD using the project vocabulary.

prompt_templates.title: Create a PRD with testing decisions
prompt_templates.scenario: Engineering planning with clear validation needs
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the to-prd skill to create a PRD. Include implementation decisions, testable modules, and testing decisions based on existing patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a triage-ready PRD
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for issue tracker publication
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the to-prd skill to create a triage-ready PRD from current context. Confirm the module plan, then publish it with the needs-triage label.

output_examples.input: Create a PRD for the checkout improvements we discussed.
output_examples.output: A product requirements document with problem statement, solution, extensive user stories, implementation decisions, testing decisions, out of scope items, and further notes.

output_examples.input: Turn this feature plan into an issue for triage.
output: A PRD written from current conversation and repository context.
A module plan that the user can confirm.
An issue tracker entry labeled needs-triage.

best_practices: Review the PRD before allowing publication to an issue tracker.
Provide issue tracker and triage label details before using the skill.
Share relevant architectural decisions and glossary terms in the conversation.
anti_patterns: Do not use it when the conversation contains secrets or private customer data.
Do not expect it to invent product requirements that were never discussed.
Do not rely on it for exact implementation file paths or code snippets.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a product requirements document from current conversation and codebase context.

faq.question: Can it publish the PRD?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill instructs the agent to publish the PRD to the project issue tracker.

faq.question: Does it ask follow-up questions?
faq.answer: It avoids a full interview, but it asks the user to confirm module and testing choices.

faq.question: What sections are included?
faq.answer: The template includes problem statement, solution, user stories, implementation decisions, testing decisions, out of scope, and notes.

faq.question: Does it include code snippets?
faq.answer: No. The skill tells the agent not to include specific file paths or code snippets.

faq.question: What should I check before publishing?
faq.answer: Check that the PRD contains no secrets, private customer data, or internal details unsuitable for the issue tracker.
